observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Inventory Committee to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2023. Henceforth, conduct the physical count during the year. Relative to this, strict monitoring and control shall be made for the issuances and procurement from the actual date of the count to the December 31 cut-off date for inclusion in the final report to be submitted on or before January 31 of the ensuing year; and
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Prioritize the strict implementation and enforcement of the documentary and procedural requirements of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the nature of the unreconciled balance of ₱4.645 million noted on the “Accounts Payable” account, prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, ensure that the FS are free from errors due to inappropriate posting;
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose as contingent liability in the Notes to FS all the duly executed contracts as of year-end totaling ₱13.735 million representing goods and services that are yet to be delivered;
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, instruct the GSO-Designate and Municipal Engineer, as applicable, to provide the Municipal Accountant a list of duly delivered items as of year-end, with its equivalent amount per POs/contracts. Accordingly, the Municipal Accountant to prepare an entry to recognize the corresponding asset and liability account based from the report thereon;
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in the Notes to FS the contingent liability arising from the undelivered POs at year-end to ensure the fair presentation of the FS.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to investigate the nature of the abnormal balances noted on the "Cash on Local Treasury" and "Due to BIR" accounts, prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, ensure that the financial statements are free from errors due to inappropriate posting.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented | 1. Investigated the nature of the abnormal balances noted on the "Cash in Local Treasury" and "Due to BIR" accounts and corrected errors of posting made.

observations.recommendation: Direct the implementing offices to refrain from proposing projects that are fully composed of labor cost which is contrary to the intent of the LGC. Accordingly, require the reviewing bodies, the Local Finance Committee (LFC) and the Local Sanggunian, to rigorously evaluate the propriety, composition, accuracy and completeness of each Department’s budget proposals, to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented out of the 20% DF; and
observations.managementAction: Not implemented.

observations.recommendation: Required implementing office, in coordination with bids and awards committee, to adapt a more dynamic procurement strategy aligned with weather forecast and historical data on weather patterns in the locality to ensure the sufficiency and immediate availability of LDRRM related items, when needed. accordingly, for welfare food items, management shall end the board to enter into framework agreement with prospective suppliers and “call off” maybe executed and issued to the winning supplier immediately upon the determination that an item is needed, pursuant to the guidelines under annex 32 of the revised implementing rules and regulations of republic act number 9184 otherwise known as the government procurement reform act. with this, lean, and just in time procurements are achieved, and the limited storage facilities of the LGU can be maximized.
observations.managementAction: Not implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the records and make necessary adjustments, as warranted, to present the correct Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current account at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation: Ensure that PPAs funded out of the DRRM funds are well-planned and meticulously reviewed by the LDRRMC. Accordingly, intensive procurement planning and coordination with stakeholders shall be conducted prior to the inclusion of PPAs in the LDRRMP and LDRRMFIP;
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle immediately the disallowances as well as ND with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS;
Perform cash cut-off procedures to ensure the completeness of transactions to be included in the FS; and
Promptly coordinate with the bank to obtain the necessary source documents or transaction details related to unidentified bank credits, in order to facilitate the proper classification and timely adjustment of reconciling items in the books.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that RCD are prepared for all undeposited cash collections as of December 31 and furnish the reports to the Accounting Office for proper recognition.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Accounting Office to conduct review and assessment of the dormant “Loans Receivable-Others” and “Other Receivable” account and record an allowance for impairment with the following entries:
Trust Fund
Account Code(cid:9)Account Titles(cid:9)Debit(cid:9)Credit
5-05-03-020 Impairment Loss – Loans and Receivables(cid:9)15,000.00
1-03-01-991 Allowance for Impairment – Loans Receivable – Others(cid:9)15,000.00
5-05-03-060(cid:9)Impairment Loss - Other Receivables(cid:9)7,800.00
1-03-01-991 Allowance for Impairment – Other Receivables(cid:9)7,800.00
Demand from the concerned official the immediate and full liquidation of the outstanding CAs aged below 10 years; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly review and monitor the status of the receivable accounts and observe the guidelines and procedures in the proper disposition of dormant accounts as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the reported balances of affected accounts in the FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Instruct the Municipal Assessor’s Office, Municipal Engineering Office and General Services Office to initiate efforts to locate the supporting documents as basis in recording the buildings identified to have no value in the NFS. Otherwise, institute an Appraisal Committee to determine the value of these buildings with consideration of their current condition and remaining useful life.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to create a subsidiary ledger card for the breeding stocks based on the result of the physical count and reappraisal, prepare the necessary adjusting entries, as warranted and submit the JEVs to the Office of the Auditor; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosures in the NFS regarding the kinds of animals included in the “Breeding Stocks” account and the causes of movement thereof whether due to changes in fair value, additional purchase/offspring, and/or death.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare working paper adjustment entries for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2024 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2025 comparative FS; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to review the nature of each transaction using the RCALGU as reference including those accounts added as prescribed under COA Circular 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to withhold the ₱5,510.14 from future income payments of the identified suppliers and remit the same to the BIR; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to follow the correct computation of taxes on income payments to suppliers by including the liquidated damages to the gross amounts payable to the suppliers to ensure that an accurate amount of taxes is withheld and remitted, pursuant to the provisions of the NIRC, as amended.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the PHIC to validate and reconcile the outstanding premium contributions amounting ₱20,039.00, including the details of the applicable employees, corresponding payroll periods, and supporting documents; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure proper accounting and disclosure of all outstanding obligations due to PhilHealth in the NFS, in accordance with applicable accounting and financial reporting standards.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the MDRRMC and the LDC to ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by providing rigid review and evaluation, especially during the preparation of the budget proposal; and
Require the MDRRMC to conduct regular review and monitoring of the implementation of PPAs and update the PPMP as necessary to ensure that funds are fully utilized. Relative to this, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of program, projects and activities to ensure that the fund’s purpose of strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters are satisfactorily attained.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the MDRRMC and the LDC to ensure that all PPAs are meticulously and judiciously planned by providing rigid review and evaluation, especially during the preparation of the budget proposal; and
Require the MDRRMC to conduct regular review and monitoring of the implementation of PPAs and update the PPMP as necessary to ensure that funds are fully utilized. Relative to this, strictly ensure the effective and efficient implementation of program, projects and activities to ensure that the fund’s purpose of strengthening the agency’s capabilities in mitigating, responding, and recovering from the impact of disasters are satisfactorily attained.

We commended the efforts of Management, through the Municipal GAD Focal person and other concerned officials, for the efficient utilization of the GAD Funds for CY 2024, thereby addressing the related gender issues and concerns identified in the GPB. We recommended that Management continuously monitor the implementation of GAD-related PPAs to sustain a high utilization rate, thereby maximizing the benefits for the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to inform and require the concerned payees to submit the stale checks issued totaling ₱51,361.80 for possible replacement. 

Accordingly, an adjusting entry shall be prepared to revert the same to the Payable accounts; and
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented | 1 Informed all concerned payees regarding their stale checks, however, some payees could not be contacted. Agency is looking for ways to identify and reach them directly.

2. Prepared adjusting entries to revert stale checks.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly reconcile the cash balance as stated in the book with the balance in bank statements, determine valid reconciling item, communicate with the bank for any error attributable with them, and prepare the adjusting or correcting entries, as warranted.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Advise the Agency’s Inventory Committee together with the representatives from the end-user units and the General Services Office (GSO) to conduct an inventory count on all office supplies that are yet to be consumed as of a cut-off date to be determined by the Committee and prepare a RPCI for recording and control purposes. Henceforth, conduct the required physical count and submit to the Auditor the report thereon not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Based on the RPCI prepared, the Municipal Accountant shall prepare an entry to recognize the cost of Inventories at hand and henceforth observe the proper accounting policies and entries in the recording of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Immediately revert the long outstanding Accounts Payable amounting to ₱0.892 million to the unappropriated surplus. Accordingly, a journal entry should be prepared by debiting “Accounts Payable” and crediting “Government Equity” accounts for the said amount;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to reverse the original entry made for the non-perfected contracts as of year-end totaling ₱3.732 million by debiting the “Accounts Payable” and crediting the corresponding asset account;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to recognize the completely delivered items totaling ₱0.422 million by debiting the corresponding asset and crediting “Accounts Payable” account;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to effect the undelivered portion of POs totaling ₱1.809 million by debiting “Accounts Payable” and crediting the corresponding asset account. Accordingly, the same amount shall be disclosed as contingent liability in the Notes to FS;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation with the Management of directing the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated FS.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented | 1. Investigated the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts.
2. Prepared an adjusting entry

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the accounting policies set forth under Item IV of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015 and prepare an adjusting entry to reflect the understatement of computed depreciation in the comparative FS. Accordingly, working paper adjustment entries shall be prepared for the restatement of the affected accounts to present the correct CY 2022 restated figures in the preparation of the CY 2023 comparative FS.
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented | 1. Reviewed and listed all Road Network projects of the LGU and requested project costs
2. Prepared and posted computed depreciation
3. Continue to correct listing of road networks, identify depreciable component and compute for the its depreciation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Continue the good practices in the implementation of Infra-related PPAs to enable maximum utilization of the 20% DF as of year-end;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer together with the concerned implementing offices, in coordination with the LFC, to conduct a periodic review and assessment of the financial and physical status of the PPAs to determine on a timely basis the possible declaration of savings which will be reappropriated for other development projects under the 20% DF, and formulate catch-up plans for slow-moving PPAs to avoid further delays in the implementation.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing office, in coordination with Bid and Awards Committee (BAC) and Budget Office, to review the reasonableness of PPAs costing to preclude series of failure of bidding, thus expedite the procurement activities and reducing the risk of delayed or non-implementation;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented | BAC reviews all PRs to reduce failure of bidding due to ABC

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant, to continue to prioritize accurate and timely determination of the LDRRM fund prior years' unxpended balances. Accordingly, the LDRRM Officer, shall ensure timely consensus with the MDRRM Council relative to the allocation of these balances to identified PPAs. Henceforth, ensure operationalization of the identified PPAs during the year by supplemental budget and for subsequent approval of the Sangguniang Bayan through an ordinance, thus, achieving optimal utilization of LDRRM Funds;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Discontinue the utilization of LDRRMF for activities or events that do not align with the primary purpose of addressing disaster related needs of the LGU. Accordingly, the LDRRM officer, show maintain a monitoring of the supplies preposition to the implementing offices and conduct a periodic review and validation of its utilization.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Utilize the stock card in the inventory management to ensure that welfare goods are available and in optimal condition when required. Similarly, ensure the timely and proper disposal of nearly expiring goods or items through the identified modality by the LGU, to preclude wastage of government funds;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Henceforth, require the BAC to submit the proof of validation on the technical, legal and financial capability of all suppliers on all purchases made through Alternative Mode of Procurement to support their eligibility; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Discontinue the practices which contravene the very purpose of the Procurement Law and its related issuances.  The canvasser and other personnel involved in the procurement process shall strictly observe the procedural guidelines set forth under the Item “8-Small Value Procurement” of the Annex H of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, to preclude issuance of notice of suspension/ disallowance. Accordingly, Management shall endeavor, to undertake comprehensive re-evaluation of the LGU’s procurement framework and implement more stringent oversight mechanisms to prevent the recurrence of similar practices.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Intensify the procurement planning by conducting trend analysis to determine activity requirements specifically on the meals and snacks and consider entering into a Framework Agreement, as allowed in Appendix 32, 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 following the procedures for competitive bidding;
observations.managementAction:
Fully Implemented | 1.Conducted comparison of prices of Basco and Uyugan (Centro) suppliers.
2.Discontinued reimbursement of snacks and meals charged to Representation expenses.
3. Ensured that all meals and snacks procured are included in PPMP and APP.

observations.recommendation: Adopt the implementation of EPA for the public bidding on the procurement of meals and snacks under a Framework Agreement to cover annually recuring activities such as Management Committee meetings, quarterly meetings of Gender and Development (GAD) Committee, and other similar activities determinable by Management;
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: Refrain from the practice of reimbursement of representation expenses except for exceptional cases such as: (a) Section 53.2 Negotiated Procurement thru Emergency Cases and (b) Per diems in connection to official travels; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Ensure that procurement of meals and snacks are included in the concerned office’s PPMP and ultimately the agency’s Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and the proof of which shall be attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management committed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to analyze the nature of every transaction and withhold the correct amount of tax on income payments to suppliers pursuant to the provisions of the NIRC, as amended and other BIR issuances and remit the same to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Revisit the supply and property management system of the agency and require the unit supply officers to render report as regards the issuances and consumption of the inventories within their respective offices to be submitted to the GSO/Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant for proper recording;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented | EO has been released for the property custodians per office, indicating their responsibilities

observations.recommendation: Require the proper utilization of the stock cards at the office level in the monitoring of supplies and materials; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Ensure that all properties of the government are properly safeguarded against deterioration, loss and wastage.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented | All supplies are properly stored per office and locked at the temporary GSO Stock Rooms

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure the conduct of semi-annual physical count of unissued inventories and reconciliation of accounting and supply records. Relative to this, the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant and the GSO-Designate to investigate the non-moving inventory accounts totaling ₱1.510 million and prepare duly supported adjusting entries, as warranted to reflect the correct balances of the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the Inventory Committee and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and the PPE records of the Accounting Office. Investigate and evaluate the differences, adjust the records as warranted. Henceforth, a reconciliation with the Accounting Office shall be made prior to the submission of the report to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the implementing offices to propose projects that are well-planned and procurement-and implementation-ready. Henceforth, rigid screening of PPAs to be funded out of the 20% DF shall be employed to ensure that only relevant, responsive and feasible items are to be funded and implemented;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the implementing offices, in coordination with Bids and Awards Committee, to observe the timely procurement activities to ensure prompt delivery of materials and other items necessary in the implementation of PPAs, accordingly, Management shall endeavor to conduct early procurement activities pursuant to the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Reform Act.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Develop substitute programs, project and activities (PPAs) in case of unforeseen circumstances that may hinder the implementation of originally planned PPA included in the GAD Plan and Budget, and strictly follow the process flow of approval and endorsement of the updated GAD Plan and Budget as prescribed under Section C.7 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-01; and
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere to the timely submission of the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report as prescribed under Item V of COA Circular 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the Local Disaster Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to review the existing PPAs under the LDRRMF to determine the relevance and responsiveness of the projects to the present circumstances including the reasonableness of project costing and make the necessary revisions as warranted subject to the appropriate approvals;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Strictly monitor the implementation of the PPAs to facilitate the timely completion thereof;
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: Direct the parties involved in the preparation of the LDRRM budget to observe the documentary requirements and procedures prescribed in COA Circular No.  2012-002 for the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management dispose all unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from the disposal.
observations.managementAction: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the conduct of an annual inventory of the local roads, observe the accounting and reporting guidelines prescribed by COA Circular 2015-008. and the General Service Officer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively and to reconcile all the reports prepared.
observations.reason: No report was submitted for 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office adhere strictly the rules and regulations mentioned above in the initial recognition of Inventory accounts in order to reflect the correct amount of Inventory and Expense accounts in the financial statement.
observations.reason: Upon validation, inventories are released altogether to end-users. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management dispose all unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from the disposal.
observations.reason: Unserviceable properties are yet to be disposed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation and submission of the required Bank Reconciliation Statements on all the funds of the Agency and make the necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of Cash in Bank account, as warranted.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the difference between the “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” Accounts and prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, prepare consolidation working papers and entries to eliminate in full the balances of reciprocal accounts in the Agency’s consolidated Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to investigate the nature of the abnormal balances noted on the asset and liability accounts, prepare an adjusting entry duly supported by verifiable evidence/analysis and henceforth, ensure that the financial statements are free from errors due to inappropriate posting.

We recommended that Management:
provide an explanation/justification with verifiable basis why the payment of Calamity/Pandemic Relief Assistance for CYs 2020 and 2021 which was noted as non-compliant with existing laws and COA rules and regulations, as mentioned above, should not be disallowed in post-audit. Otherwise, require the immediate refund of the amount received by all payees; and
discontinue granting benefits to its employees without sufficient and valid legal basis.

We recommended that Management require the identified officials and employees to file an application for bonding with the Bureau of the Treasury-Batanes Provincial Office, strictly monitor the expiration of bond coverage and apply for renewal thereof prior to its expiration as required in Treasury Circular No. 02-2019 dated April 25, 2019 with due consideration on the processing time from the application to the approval of the bond.

We recommended that Management:
direct all individual service providers under Job Order who are deriving income below P250,00.00 to execute an Income Payee’s Sworn Declaration of gross receipts/sales (Annex B2 of RR 11-2018 or Annex A1 of RMC 51-2018) and pay the Annual Registration Fee the proofs of which shall be submitted to the Municipal Human Resource Management Office;
require the authorized representative of the Agency, after submission by the service providers of the duly accomplished declaration, to execute the Income Payor/Withholding Agent’s Sworn Declaration (Annex C of RR 11-2018) and submit together with the list of payees who shall not be subjected to withholding taxes to the Bureau of Internal Revenue; and
require the Municipal Human Resource Office to strictly observe the annual compliance deadlines as prescribed by RR 11-2018. Accordingly, the requirements shall be submitted by the individual service providers before January 15 or prior to initial income payment, whichever is applicable. The documentary requirements of the Agency as withholding agent shall be submitted to the BIR  office on or before the last day of January of each year or on the fifteenth (15th) day of the following month when a new income recipient submitted the payee’s sworn declaration to the lone income payor/withholding agent.

We recommended Management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.
observations.reason: The unliquidated cash advance pertain to officials and employees who are no longer connected to the Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement the priority projects funded from national agencies and monitor the utilization of the funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement (MOA) with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining unutilized amount on programs/projects which have already been completed should be returned to the source agency or as agreed upon in the MOA.
observations.reason: The balance of Due to NGAs pertains to fund transfers for ongoing projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned officer to insure all of the provincial properties particularly the motor vehicles with the General Insurance Fund of GSIS, otherwise provide/submit necessary documents to justify the action of not insuring some of the motor vehicles.
observations.reason: Nine Motor vehicles have been insured with the GSIS. The remaining two are unserviceable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle immediately the disallowances as well as ND with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
observations.reason: Settlements totaling P48,238.00 were made during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to continue to disbursed funds received from national agency in accordance to the purpose of the project/activities/program. Likewise the Management must validate carefully the beneficiaries/recipients as included in the Social Amelioration Card (SAC) as per Item VI-B of DSWD MC No. 09.
observations.reason: Management implemented our recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to expedite the implementation of all priority development projects and activities for timely realization of the expected benefits that could be derived thereat by the constituents. Likewise, APP, POW and Work and Financial Plan must be prepared and submitted to offices concerned including COA.
observations.reason: Several projects funded out of the 20% DF are being implemented. The pandemic which caused mobility restrictions further contributed to the delay in implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the concerned resource persons who were recipients of the financial assistance totaling to P80,000.00 to submit the supporting documents stated above as required under Item 1.2.4.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure that the required supporting documents of all claims are complete before payment.
observations.managementAction: Still in the process of gathering documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to strictly monitor and cause the liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds in compliance to Section 89 of P.D. 1445, and the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 96-004 and 97-002 is enjoined.

We recommended that Management implement the priority projects funded from national agencies and monitor the utilization of the funds by ensuring compliance with the memorandum of agreement (MOA) with the source agency. Likewise, the remaining unutilized amount on programs/projects which have already been completed should be returned to the source agency or as agreed upon in the MOA.

We recommended that in the conduct of an annual inventory of the local roads, observe the accounting and reporting guidelines prescribed by COA Circular 2015-008. and the General Service Officer and Accountant maintain the required Local Road Network Property and Ledger Cards, respectively and to reconcile all the reports prepared.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officer to insure all of the provincial properties particularly the motor vehicles with the General Insurance Fund of GSIS, otherwise provide/submit necessary documents to justify the action of not insuring some of the motor vehicles.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive encourage the government officials and employees to settle immediately the disallowances as well as ND with COA Order of Execution and adhere strictly with the provisions of COA Memorandum No. 2009-084 dated November 16, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend Management to continue to disbursed funds received from national agency in accordance to the purpose of the project/activities/program. Likewise the Management must validate carefully the beneficiaries/recipients as included in the Social Amelioration Card (SAC) as per Item VI-B of DSWD MC No. 09.

We recommended that the Accounting Office adhere strictly the rules and regulations mentioned above in the initial recognition of Inventory accounts in order to reflect the correct amount of  Inventory and Expense accounts in the financial statement.

We recommended that management to direct the designated property officer to facilitate the conduct of physical count of property in order to come up with the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment.       advances to avoid misuse of government funds. Send demand  letters  to

   10.11 We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive will consider

observations.recommendation: We recommended that LDRRMF appropriation must be properly allocated for the 30% QRF in lump-sum and 70% disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management has observed the proper allocation of the QRF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the acting Municipal Accountant prepare a journal entry voucher to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in a Special Trust Fund in order to ensure and facilitate the continued use of the fund for disaster risk reduction and management activities as provided in the LDRRMFIP within the next five years.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Accountant has transferred the LDRRMF to the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management adhere strictly to the provisions of RA 9003. Likewise, prepare detailed Work and Financial Plan of the budgeted SWMP.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted the SWMP to the EMB however they did not submit required Work and Financial Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management and BAC strictly implement the provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July 12, 2012 on the procurement through shopping. Sale of bid docs shall apply only to public bidding method of procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the corrective measures in the procurement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions of RA 9184. Bidding documents prescribed by the GPPB must be implemented. Likewise, all supporting documents as required in the provision of the COA Circular No. 2012-001 must be strictly adhered.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented the use of the prescribed Bidding Documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal control in the disbursement of public funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already implemented the observation in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that liable/responsible for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle their accounts within the timeframe as prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: The Management has been paying in an installment basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor and cause immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds. Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, send demand letters to accountable officers for the submission of liquidation reports and to refund to the Treasurer’s office any unused cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Management has settled previous obligations however Management is continuously granting cash advances even with previous cash advance granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant maintain ledger cards for each commodity/stock and PPE following the above-cited regulation. Likewise, the purchase of supplies and materials should be recorded as inventories upon acquisition and to the related expense account upon issuance of inventories based on SSMI.
observations.managementAction: The OIC Municipal Accountant is recording the acquisition of supplies as inventories and the related expense upon issuance. However, no SSMI is being prepared.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended management to strictly  follow  the  provisions  of
        Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular No.  2012-001  and  Section  309,  Title
        Five, Chapter II, Article One of Republic Act 7160 in  the  receipt
        and recording of funds which officially come into the possession of
        the local government as trustee, agent or administrator.

2.5 We recommended that the municipality expedite  the  implementation
        of all priority development projects and activities  for  a  timely
        realization of the expected benefits that could be derived  thereat
        by the constituents. Likewise, APP, POW and Work and Financial Plan
        must be prepared and submitted to offices concerned including COA.

3.3 We recommended that management prepare and submit  the  Report  on
        the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Auditor for  proper
        review and evaluation.

4.3  We  recommended  that  LDRRMF  appropriation  must  be   properly
        allocated for the 30% QRF in lump-sum and 70% disaster  mitigation,
        prevention  and  preparedness  with  details  as  to  projects  and
        activities.

5.7 We recommended that the  acting  Municipal  Accountant  prepare  a
        journal  entry  voucher  to  transfer  the   unexpended/unobligated
        balance of the LDRRMF in a Special Trust Fund in  order  to  ensure
        and facilitate the continued use of  the  fund  for  disaster  risk
        reduction and management activities as  provided  in  the  LDRRMFIP
        within the next five years.

6.6 We recommended that management adhere strictly to  the  provisions
        of RA 9003. Likewise, prepare detailed Work and Financial  Plan  of
        the budgeted SWMP.

7.4  We  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct   the
        department heads to prepare their  Project  Procurement  Management
        Plan (PPMP) to  be  submitted  to  the  procuring  entity’s  Budget
        Officer for evaluation, if warranted, forward to the  Head  of  the
        procuring entity for approval. Then furnish a copy of the procuring
        entity’s budget proposal as well as the corresponding PPMPs to  the
        BAC Secretary for consolidation  to  the  Annual  Procurement  Plan
        (APP). Furthermore, the APP must be consistent with their  approved
        budget for the year.

8.7 We recommended that management  and  BAC  strictly  implement  the
        provisions of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated July  12,
        2012 on the procurement through shopping. Sale of  bid  docs  shall
        apply only to public bidding method of procurement.

9.5 We recommended that management strictly implement  the  provisions
        of RA 9184. Bidding  documents  prescribed  by  the  GPPB  must  be
        implemented. Likewise, all supporting documents as required in  the
        provision of  the  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001  must  be  strictly
        adhered.

10.8 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant strictly  adhere
        with the provisions of COA  Circular  No.  2009-006  in  submitting
        accounts and other reports of the  municipality.  Likewise,  submit
        the required reports within the prescribed period in order to avoid
        instituting  administrative  disciplinary  action  as  required  by
        Section 39 (1) & (3) of PD 1445.


      10.9 We further  recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  will
        consider to open the position of Municipal Accountant  with  person
        possessing  the  qualifications  as  provided   under   the   Local
        Government Code in order to improve the financial reporting  system
        of the Municipality.

11.3 We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to  strictly  comply  with
         the provisions of COA Circular 92-389 to maintain a sound internal
         control in the disbursement of public funds.

16.6 We recommended the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  reconcile  the
        noted difference in the reciprocal  account  and  effect  necessary
        adjustments to reflect the correct account balance as of year-end.

17.7 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant  maintain ledger
        cards  for each commodity/stock and PPE following  the  above-cited
        regulation. Likewise,   the  purchase  of  supplies  and  materials
        should be recorded as  inventories  upon  acquisition  and  to  the
        related expense account upon issuance of inventories based on RSMI.

We recommended that the Accounting Office strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 in submitting accounts and other reports of the municipality. Likewise, submit the required reports within the prescribed period in order to avoid instituting administrative disciplinary action as required by Section 39 (1) & (3) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: AAR  ‘15 p. 22 The LCE directed the OIC-Accountant to submit reports as required in the regulations cited. AAR ‘15 p. 23 Succeeding reports already reflected the correct figures
observations.reason: Laxity of the OIC-Accountant to perform her duties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that reports of the agency on government projects reflect the actual costs incurred. The accounting, budget and engineering offices should reconcile their reports to reflect the true and actual balance of their accomplishment as of a given date.
We recommended that balance of said downloaded fund from PDAF and all other downloaded fund from the same fund should be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.
observations.managementAction: AAR  ‘15 p. 24 The LGU no longer received any PDAF during the year, thus, no more expenditures were made.
observations.reason: The balance was not yet returned to the National Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit to the Auditor the MOA on all downloaded funds together with the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents to facilitate review of the liquidation reports.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted LR for fund transfer received for 2014 but not yet for 2015.
observations.reason: No LR was submitted for transferred fund received for 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of the MOA as agreed upon between the municipality and the PGB.
We recommend management to cause the timely preparation and submission of the report required in the COA circular.

We recommended that management adhere to the provision of RA 9003. Likewise, prepare detailed Work and Financial Plan of the SWMP. Management must include in its Annual Investment Plan projects or activities that converts its open dumpsite compliant to the provisions of the said government regulation.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: SWMP of the municipality had not been approved and implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management follow strictly the provisions of DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01 dated April 13, 2011. Likewise, prioritize the programs, activities and projects to obtain the benefits as envisioned under the said fund.
observations.managementAction: Stopped funding of projects outside the criteria set forth in the mentioned JMC

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor and cause immediate liquidation of all outstanding cash advances to avoid misuse of government funds. Comply strictly with the provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Likewise, send demand letters to accountable officers for the submission of liquidation reports and to refund to the Treasurer any unused cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The LCE demanded settlement of unliquidated CAs
observations.reason: Some AOs with unliquidated CA are no longer attached to the LGU thus demand cannot be made while some who are still in service failed to comply with the demand of the LCE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management dispose all unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and to generate additional income from the disposal.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory was conducted at the beginning of the year and identified the unserviceable assets.
observations.reason: No report was made by the Inventory Team on the result of the actual physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management submit complete supporting documents as required by the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 to verify the necessity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Management already started the implemented of the project whose funding was downloaded by the DENR
observations.reason: APP was not yet prepared for infra projects and POWs and MOA of some infra projects are not yet submitted to COA

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the persons liable/responsible for the audit disallowances and suspensions to settle their accounts within the timeframe as prescribed by law.
observations.managementAction: The LCE commanded all employees of the LGU with NDs to settle their accounts.
observations.reason: Only partial settlement of NDs and NS were made through salary deduction and submission of documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to follow strictly the provisions of Section 13.1 to 13.3, Rule V of the RIRR of RA 9184. Observers shall be invited in writing at least three (3) days before the date of the procurement stage/activity and shall come from duly registered organizations to enhance transparency of the process.
observations.managementAction: BAC submitted invitation to the office of the ATL during opening of bids
observations.reason: Invitation received by the Office of the ATL was only during opening of bids and not on all BAC activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the minimum requirement provided by Section 48 of the General Appropriations Act of 2015. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to reflect in the certification the correct net take home of employees when they apply for loans. Stop granting additional loans to employees whose net take home pay fall below the minimum requirement as provided by the above regulations.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the officers in charge to implement said regulation
observations.reason: Some net pay of employees still do not meet the minimum requirement due to payment of loans and disallowances but management said that grant of loan is now stricter and see to it that net take home pay will no longer be below minimum requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the provisions of the regulations mentioned above and to observe due prudence in the utilization of its meager resources.
observations.managementAction: Management no longer grant donation to private organization
